figures.caption: Figure 2: Two implementations on VLMs are considered: reconstructing visual tokens and performing direct inference via a fine-tuned adapter.
Table 2: Reconstruction performance comparison on CIFAR-100 and SVHN. For recent baselines and PADL, results are reported as mean ± standard deviation over 10 repeated runs.
Figure 5: PSNR comparison on CIFAR-100 and SVHN under varying dictionary activation levels.

Table 5: Performance comparison on image VQA benchmarks. PADL is used as a visualrepresentation compression module. PADL denotes the version without adapter fine-tuning, while PADL+ denotes the version with adapter fine-tuning.
Table 6: Performance comparison on video reasoning benchmarks.
Table 7: High-level comparison of representative dictionary-learning formulations.

Table 10: Comparison with recent sparse-representation baselines on CIFAR-100 and SVHN. Results are reported as mean and standard deviation over 10 repeated runs. Lower RMSE is better; higher PSNR and SSIM are better.

Table 13: Optimization behavior of the three objective terms on CIFAR-100 and SVHN. The objective terms generally decrease and stabilize during training, suggesting stable empirical optimization behavior.
